headline: Montana Volunteers Celebrate Sober Choices With Surprise Coffee Cards
meta_description: Montana volunteers leave $5 gift cards on cars left overnight at bars, rewarding drivers who chose not to drive drunk.
summary_short: The Montana Barfairies surprise responsible bar patrons with coffee gift cards—a viral movement that's turned one act of kindness into a nationwide conversation.
body:
On a cold Montana morning, you wake up to discover something unexpected tucked under your truck's windshield wiper—not a ticket, but a $5 gift card for coffee. It's a small reward for a big decision: choosing not to drive home drunk the night before.

This is the work of the Montana Barfairies, volunteers who have turned a simple act of kindness into a movement gaining national attention. What began as leaving coffee gift cards on cars parked overnight at Flathead Valley bars has expanded into something larger: a nonprofit advocating for stronger DUI laws and supporting families affected by drunk driving.

The commitment required is real. Volunteers wake before dawn—sometimes in February cold—to leave their own money behind for strangers. But the response shows the idea resonates. When Jesse, Beverly, Grace, and Kate posted a TikTok video of rewarding bar patrons from the Whitefish Winter Carnival, it garnered 142,000 views.

In Polson, Tim and Kelly left cards at 8 vehicles. In Kalispell, Shannon and Brittainy distributed 20 gift cards totaling $100 on a single freezing morning. Each video drew millions of views, with commenters celebrating not just the generosity, but the brilliance of rewarding the choice that matters most: staying safe.
